assertion_a: The Borrower shall not incur additional Indebtedness exceeding $5,000,000 in the aggregate.
assertion_b: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Borrower may incur Permitted Indebtedness as defined in Section 6.02 of this Agreement.
notes: Loan covenant + explicit carve-out; current judge prompt likely false-positives.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not make any Restricted Payment.
assertion_b: The Borrower may make Restricted Payments in an aggregate amount not to exceed $1,000,000 per fiscal year.

assertion_a: The Company shall not sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of any Material Asset.
assertion_b: Sales of Inventory in the ordinary course of business shall not constitute a Disposition for purposes of this Section.
notes: General prohibition + definitional carve-out via 'shall not constitute'.

assertion_a: No Subsidiary shall guarantee any Indebtedness of the Borrower.
assertion_b: A Subsidiary may guarantee Indebtedness of the Borrower if such Indebtedness is Permitted Subordinated Indebtedness.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not enter into any merger, consolidation, or amalgamation.
assertion_b: The Borrower may consolidate with or merge into any Wholly-Owned Subsidiary, provided that the Borrower is the surviving entity.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not make any acquisition of any other Person.
assertion_b: The Borrower may make a Permitted Acquisition, provided the aggregate consideration does not exceed $25,000,000 in any fiscal year.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not declare or pay any dividends on its Equity Interests.
assertion_b: The Borrower may declare dividends payable solely in additional Equity Interests of the same class.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not amend its Organizational Documents in any manner.
assertion_b: The Borrower may amend its Organizational Documents to effect a change that is not adverse to the interests of the Lenders.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not create or suffer to exist any Lien on any of its property.
assertion_b: Permitted Liens as defined in Section 7.01 are expressly excluded from the foregoing restriction.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not engage in any business other than the Specified Business.
assertion_b: The Borrower may engage in any business reasonably related or incidental to the Specified Business.

assertion_a: All employees shall complete the annual compliance training by December 31 of each year.
assertion_b: Employees on approved leave during the training period shall complete the training within 30 days of returning to work.

assertion_a: Employees may take up to 15 days of paid time off per calendar year.
assertion_b: Probationary employees, defined as those within their first 90 days of employment, may take up to 5 days of paid time off.
notes: General PTO permission + probationary sub-population cap.

assertion_a: All employees are eligible for the company-sponsored health insurance plan beginning on their first day of employment.
assertion_b: Part-time employees scheduled for fewer than 20 hours per week are not eligible for the company-sponsored health insurance plan.

assertion_a: Employees must submit expense reports within 30 days of incurring the expense.
assertion_b: Field sales employees may submit consolidated monthly expense reports within 45 days of the month in which expenses were incurred.

assertion_a: All employees are required to work from the office a minimum of three days per week.
assertion_b: Employees holding the title of Remote Engineer are exempt from in-office attendance requirements.

assertion_a: Employees may not accept gifts from clients valued in excess of $50.
assertion_b: Gifts received in the context of approved client appreciation events organized by the Company are exempt from the foregoing restriction.

assertion_a: All employees shall provide two weeks' written notice prior to resignation.
assertion_b: Employees in their probationary period may resign with one week's written notice.

assertion_a: Performance reviews shall be conducted annually for all employees.
assertion_b: Newly hired employees shall receive an initial performance review six months after their start date, and annually thereafter.

assertion_a: Bonuses are awarded to all employees based on annual performance evaluations.
assertion_b: Employees who are on a Performance Improvement Plan at the time of bonus determination are not eligible for the annual bonus.

assertion_a: All employees must use the company-issued laptop for work-related tasks.
assertion_b: Contractors and temporary workers may use their own equipment subject to security review and approval.

assertion_a: No employee shall access customer financial data without explicit authorization.
assertion_b: Employees in the Compliance department are authorized to access customer financial data for the purpose of regulatory audits.
notes: Access prohibition + standing authorization for Compliance.

assertion_a: All sales contracts must be reviewed and signed by a Vice President or higher.
assertion_b: Sales contracts with a total value below $10,000 may be signed by a Sales Manager.

assertion_a: Hiring decisions require approval from the department head and Human Resources.
assertion_b: Internal transfers between teams reporting to the same department head do not require additional Human Resources approval.

assertion_a: Employees may not discuss confidential matters in public spaces.
assertion_b: Discussions among authorized personnel within the Company's secured premises are not considered to occur in public spaces.

assertion_a: Travel expenses exceeding $1,000 require pre-approval from the employee's manager.
assertion_b: Travel expenses for company-sponsored conferences pre-approved by the department head do not require additional individual approval.

assertion_a: All software installations on company devices require IT review.
assertion_b: Software listed on the company-approved software catalog may be installed by employees without additional IT review.

assertion_a: No employee shall represent the Company at industry events without prior authorization.
assertion_b: Members of the official spokesperson roster, as designated by the Communications department, are authorized to represent the Company at industry events.

assertion_a: Customer complaints must be escalated to a supervisor within 24 hours.
assertion_b: Routine inquiries that are resolved at the first point of contact are not considered complaints for purposes of the escalation requirement.

assertion_a: All new vendor relationships require a signed master services agreement.
assertion_b: Single-purchase vendor engagements below $5,000 may proceed under the Company's standard purchase order terms.
notes: Vendor MSA rule + low-value PO exception.

assertion_a: Use of company credit cards is restricted to approved business expenses.
assertion_b: Charges incurred by employees while on Company-approved business travel are deemed approved business expenses for purposes of this Section.
notes: Use restriction + definitional sweep carve-out for travel.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not prepay any Subordinated Indebtedness prior to the Maturity Date.
assertion_b: Mandatory prepayments required by the terms of the Subordinated Indebtedness itself are permitted under this covenant.

assertion_a: The Issuer shall not redeem any Preferred Stock prior to the Stated Redemption Date.
assertion_b: The Issuer may redeem Preferred Stock upon a Change of Control as defined in the Certificate of Designations.

assertion_a: No Loan Party shall make any Investment in any Person.
assertion_b: Investments in cash and Cash Equivalents are excluded from the prohibition on Investments.

assertion_a: The Company shall not amend or modify any Material Contract.
assertion_b: Amendments that are administrative in nature and do not materially alter the economic terms of the Material Contract are not subject to this restriction.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall maintain Consolidated Net Leverage Ratio of no greater than 4.0 to 1.0.
assertion_b: During any Limited Condition Transaction Test Period, the maximum Consolidated Net Leverage Ratio shall be increased by 0.5x.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not change its fiscal year.
assertion_b: The Borrower may change its fiscal year to align with the fiscal year of a newly acquired Material Subsidiary.

assertion_a: No payment shall be made to any Affiliate other than in the ordinary course of business.
assertion_b: Payments to Affiliates pursuant to a Permitted Affiliate Transaction listed on Schedule 6.05 are not restricted by this Section.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not enter into any Sale and Leaseback Transaction.
assertion_b: Sale and Leaseback Transactions involving equipment with an aggregate value of less than $500,000 per fiscal year are permitted.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not incur any obligation under a Hedging Agreement other than in the ordinary course of business and not for speculative purposes.
assertion_b: Hedging Agreements entered into for the purpose of managing interest-rate or foreign-currency risk on existing obligations are deemed to be in the ordinary course of business.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall not voluntarily terminate any Material Contract.
assertion_b: The Borrower may terminate a Material Contract if the counterparty is in material breach of its obligations thereunder.

assertion_a: All employees shall report safety incidents to their manager within 24 hours.
assertion_b: Employees may report safety incidents to the Anonymous Reporting Hotline at any time.
notes: Obligation to manager + permission for alternate channel; both can coexist.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall deliver financial statements within 45 days of fiscal quarter-end.
assertion_b: The Borrower may deliver supplemental schedules at its discretion.
notes: Different propositions (statements vs supplemental schedules); 'shall' + 'may' coexist.

assertion_a: The Lender shall provide written notice of any waiver request decision within ten Business Days.
assertion_b: The Lender may request additional collateral as a condition of any waiver.
notes: Distinct obligations and permissions; not in tension.

assertion_a: The Company shall maintain insurance coverage in an amount not less than $10 million.
assertion_b: The Company may purchase additional insurance coverage at its election.
notes: Floor + permission to exceed; trivially compatible.

assertion_a: Employees shall use the official expense reporting system.
assertion_b: Managers may approve expense reports submitted through alternative formats during documented system downtime.

assertion_a: Each Loan Party shall execute the Joinder Agreement.
assertion_b: Each Loan Party may designate one Authorized Representative to execute on its behalf.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall maintain Minimum Liquidity of $5,000,000.
assertion_b: The Borrower may include unused Revolving Commitments in the calculation of Minimum Liquidity.

assertion_a: All deposits shall be made to the designated operating account.
assertion_b: Deposits may be made by wire transfer or by physical check at any branch.

assertion_a: Employees shall return company equipment upon termination of employment.
assertion_b: Employees may request to purchase company equipment at fair market value upon termination.
notes: Return obligation + alternative purchase permission; not a strict override (audit 2026-05-15).

assertion_a: The Issuer shall give notice of redemption no less than 30 days prior to the Redemption Date.
assertion_b: The Issuer may give such notice by mail, email, or electronic distribution platform.

assertion_a: The Company shall maintain coverage under the Master Insurance Policy.
assertion_b: The Company shall not maintain coverage under the Master Insurance Policy.
notes: Direct shall vs shall not on same predicate.

assertion_a: Employees shall use the company-approved VPN when accessing internal systems remotely.
assertion_b: Employees shall not use the company-approved VPN; use the new Zero Trust Network Access platform exclusively.
notes: Opposing obligations on the same VPN.

assertion_a: The Borrower shall pay interest quarterly in arrears.
assertion_b: The Borrower shall not pay interest more frequently than annually.
notes: Quarterly > annual frequency; obligation conflicts with the cap.

assertion_a: The Lender shall release the collateral upon full repayment of principal.
assertion_b: The Lender shall not release the collateral until the expiration of the 90-day clawback period following final repayment.

assertion_a: The Issuer shall file the Form 10-Q within 40 days of fiscal quarter-end.
assertion_b: The Issuer shall not file the Form 10-Q within 45 days of fiscal quarter-end.
notes: 40-day window is inside the 45-day window; satisfying A violates B.

assertion_a: Tenant shall maintain renters insurance throughout the term of this Lease.
assertion_b: Tenant shall not be required to maintain renters insurance.
notes: Direct shall vs shall not on same obligation.

assertion_a: The Company shall convene the annual stockholders' meeting in person.
assertion_b: The Company shall not convene any meeting of stockholders in person.

assertion_a: All vendor invoices shall be processed through the centralized AP system.
assertion_b: Vendor invoices shall not be processed through the centralized AP system; the new Procurement Hub replaces it effective immediately.

assertion_a: The Trustee shall distribute remaining principal to Beneficiaries upon termination of the Trust.
assertion_b: The Trustee shall not distribute any principal to Beneficiaries; all assets shall revert to the Settlor.

assertion_a: All clinical staff shall complete CPR recertification annually.
assertion_b: Clinical staff shall not be required to complete CPR recertification more frequently than every two years.
notes: Annual cadence violates 'no more than every two years' ceiling.

assertion_a: Employees must obtain manager approval before booking travel exceeding $2,000.
assertion_b: Employees may not seek individual manager approval for travel; all travel bookings go through the Travel Approval Committee.

assertion_a: The Borrower must deliver financial statements within 45 days of fiscal quarter-end.
assertion_b: The Borrower may not deliver financial statements outside of the Quarterly Reporting Window of 45 to 60 days post-quarter-end.
notes: Within-45-days violates the not-before-45 window. Note: reading 'within 45 days' strictly (<45) makes this a contradiction; a <=45 reading makes day 45 a single satisfying point and reduces this to contrariety. Pair retained as a boundary-case diagnostic.

assertion_a: All new hires must complete background checks prior to their start date.
assertion_b: Background checks may not be initiated until after the candidate's start date for cost-control reasons.

assertion_a: All software contributions must be reviewed by at least two engineers.
assertion_b: Software contributions may not be reviewed by more than one engineer in order to keep review velocity high.

assertion_a: All confidential documents must be shredded before disposal.
assertion_b: Confidential documents may not be shredded; instead, deposit them in the secure disposal bin.

assertion_a: All employees must use multi-factor authentication for access to internal systems.
assertion_b: Employees may not use SMS-based authentication as a second factor due to known vulnerabilities.
notes: MFA includes more than SMS; obligation + scoped prohibition coexist.

assertion_a: The Contractor must remove all debris from the work site at the end of each business day.
assertion_b: The Contractor may not remove construction debris through the building's main elevators.

assertion_a: All meeting rooms must be vacated by 6:00pm local time.
assertion_b: Meeting rooms may not be reserved past 5:30pm without special authorization.
notes: 5:30pm reservation cap + 6:00pm vacate rule are consistent.

assertion_a: The Bank must verify identity for all transactions exceeding $10,000.
assertion_b: The Bank may not require identity verification for transactions below $10,000 from established customers.
notes: Verification at >$10k + no-verification under $10k; thresholds compatible.

assertion_a: All inventory must be physically counted by the end of each fiscal quarter.
assertion_b: Physical inventory counts may not be conducted by the same individual who handles cash receipts for the location.

assertion_a: The Company will provide each employee with a company-issued laptop.
assertion_b: Employees may decline the company-issued laptop and use a personal device subject to BYOD policy.

assertion_a: The Vendor will deliver Goods on the Delivery Date specified in the Purchase Order.
assertion_b: The Buyer may request expedited delivery for an additional expedited-handling fee.

assertion_a: The Investor will provide funding within 30 days of executing the Term Sheet.
assertion_b: The Company may extend the funding deadline by up to 60 days upon written notice to the Investor.

assertion_a: The Service Provider will perform routine maintenance during off-peak hours.
assertion_b: The Customer may request emergency maintenance during business hours at the on-call rate.

assertion_a: The Issuer will redeem the Notes on the Maturity Date.
assertion_b: The Holder may request early redemption at any time after the Optional Redemption Date.
notes: Mandatory final redemption + optional early call.

assertion_a: The Borrower will make principal payments quarterly in accordance with the Amortization Schedule.
assertion_b: The Borrower may prepay the loan in full or in part at any time without penalty.

assertion_a: The Trustee will distribute income to Beneficiaries annually.
assertion_b: Beneficiaries may request quarterly distribution upon written notice to the Trustee.

assertion_a: The Insurance Carrier will issue the Policy upon receipt of the first premium payment.
assertion_b: The Insured may request expedited issuance for an additional underwriting fee.

assertion_a: The Vendor will install the Equipment on the Installation Date specified in the Order Form.
assertion_b: The Customer may delay installation by up to 30 days upon written notice.

assertion_a: The Auditor will deliver the Audit Report no later than 90 days after fiscal year-end.
assertion_b: Management may request preliminary findings prior to the final report.

assertion_a: Employees are eligible for the 401(k) match beginning on their hire date.
assertion_b: Effective January 1, 2026, employees are eligible for the 401(k) match after 90 days of service.
notes: Different effective periods; the second supersedes the first as of 2026-01-01.

assertion_a: Software License Agreement effective 2023-01-01: customer may use the Software on up to 100 concurrent users.
assertion_b: Software License Agreement effective 2025-01-01: customer may use the Software on up to 500 concurrent users.
notes: Two contracts with distinct effective dates.

assertion_a: Prior to January 1, 2025, the Service Fee was $1,500 per month.
assertion_b: Beginning January 1, 2025, the Service Fee is $2,000 per month.

assertion_a: Lease Term: January 1, 2020 through December 31, 2024. Monthly Rent: $5,000.
assertion_b: Lease Renewal Term: January 1, 2025 through December 31, 2029. Monthly Rent: $6,500.
notes: Initial term + renewal term with stated rents.

assertion_a: Employee Handbook effective 2024-01-01: Paid Time Off accrues at 10 days per year.
assertion_b: Employee Handbook effective 2025-01-01: Paid Time Off accrues at 15 days per year.

assertion_a: The Acquisition shall close on or before March 1, 2025.
assertion_b: If the Acquisition does not close by March 1, 2025, the Termination Fee shall become payable.
notes: Closing target + contingent fee; not in conflict.

assertion_a: The Subscription Term begins on the Effective Date and continues for one year.
assertion_b: Either Party may terminate the Subscription Term on 30 days' notice after the first anniversary of the Effective Date.

assertion_a: Wages shall be paid bi-weekly, on every other Friday.
assertion_b: Effective with the pay period ending June 30, 2025, wages shall be paid weekly, on every Friday.
notes: Old cadence + new cadence with explicit effective date.

assertion_a: Tax Year 2023: standard deduction is $13,850.
assertion_b: Tax Year 2024: standard deduction is $14,600.

assertion_a: Founders' Agreement (executed March 15, 2020): each Founder is entitled to a 25% equity stake.
assertion_b: Amended and Restated Founders' Agreement (executed September 1, 2023): Founder Alice's equity stake is reduced to 20%.
notes: Original + amended-and-restated with later execution date.

assertion_a: Fiscal Year 2024 Revenue: $48,250,000.
assertion_b: Fiscal Year 2025 Revenue: $52,100,000.

assertion_a: For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, the Company reported a Net Loss of $2.1 million.
assertion_b: For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, the Company reported Net Income of $4.5 million.
notes: Distinct fiscal years; sign change is real but scoped.

assertion_a: The Borrower's Consolidated EBITDA for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 was $15.2 million.
assertion_b: The Borrower's Consolidated EBITDA for the trailing twelve months ended September 30, 2025 was $18.7 million.
notes: Different measurement periods (calendar FY vs LTM).

assertion_a: For Fiscal Year 2024, the Bonus Pool was set at 10% of pre-tax profit.
assertion_b: For Fiscal Year 2025, the Bonus Pool shall be set at 12% of pre-tax profit.

assertion_a: Capital expenditures for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 totaled $3.8 million.
assertion_b: The Capital Expenditures Budget for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2025 is $5.0 million.
notes: Actual prior year + budget for next year.

assertion_a: During Fiscal Year 2024, the Borrower maintained a Consolidated Leverage Ratio of 3.8x.
assertion_b: The Borrower shall not permit its Consolidated Leverage Ratio to exceed 4.0x during any fiscal year ending on or after December 31, 2025.
notes: Historical actual + forward-looking covenant for a different period.

assertion_a: The Company's headcount as of fiscal year-end 2024 was 142 employees.
assertion_b: The Company's headcount as of fiscal year-end 2025 was 187 employees.

assertion_a: Audit Report for Fiscal Year 2024: unqualified opinion.
assertion_b: Audit Report for Fiscal Year 2025: unqualified opinion with explanatory paragraph regarding subsequent-event disclosure.

assertion_a: Fiscal Year 2024 sales by region: North America 60%, EMEA 25%, APAC 15%.
assertion_b: Fiscal Year 2025 sales by region: North America 55%, EMEA 30%, APAC 15%.

assertion_a: The 2024 fiscal year ran from October 1, 2023 to September 30, 2024.
assertion_b: The 2025 fiscal year shall run from January 1, 2025 to December 31, 2025, reflecting the Company's calendar-year alignment.
notes: Fiscal-year definition itself shifted to calendar alignment.

assertion_a: During the initial 12-month subscription term, the Customer is not entitled to any refund.
assertion_b: Following the initial subscription term, the Customer may request a pro-rata refund upon cancellation.
notes: Two refund regimes scoped to different periods.

assertion_a: During the first year of employment, employees accrue PTO at 0.8 days per month.
assertion_b: After the first year of employment, employees accrue PTO at 1.25 days per month.

assertion_a: During the Tenant's occupancy of the Premises, the Landlord shall not enter without 24 hours' written notice.
assertion_b: Following the Tenant's vacating of the Premises, the Landlord may enter at any time for inspection or repair.

assertion_a: During the Holdback Period, $500,000 of the Purchase Price shall be retained in the Escrow Account.
assertion_b: Following expiration of the Holdback Period, all remaining Escrow funds shall be released to the Seller.

assertion_a: During the 90-day Trial Period, the Customer may terminate at any time without penalty.
assertion_b: Following the Trial Period, termination requires 30 days' written notice and is subject to early-termination fees.

assertion_a: During business hours (9:00am to 5:00pm local time), the Support Line shall be answered by a live agent.
assertion_b: Outside business hours, calls to the Support Line will be routed to voicemail for next-business-day callback.

assertion_a: During the Probationary Period, the Employee's at-will employment may be terminated for any reason.
assertion_b: After successful completion of the Probationary Period, termination requires documented cause.

assertion_a: During the period of January 1 through April 15 each year, the Tax Preparation Office shall maintain extended weekend hours.
assertion_b: Outside of tax season, weekend hours are not offered.

assertion_a: During any period in which the Borrower's Total Leverage Ratio exceeds 4.5x, the Applicable Margin shall increase by 50 basis points.
assertion_b: When the Borrower's Total Leverage Ratio is at or below 4.5x, the Applicable Margin shall be the Base Margin.

assertion_a: During the Standstill Period, no Bidder may submit a competing offer.
assertion_b: Following expiration of the Standstill Period, a Bidder may submit a new offer subject to the Board's customary review.

assertion_a: The Company completed its initial public offering on June 15, 2021.
assertion_b: Following the take-private transaction in 2024, the Company is a privately-held entity wholly owned by Acme Capital Partners.
notes: IPO event + later take-private; both true at their stated times.

assertion_a: The CFO position was held by Maria Chen from 2018 to 2022.
assertion_b: The current CFO is David Park, who joined the Company in 2023.

assertion_a: The Property was purchased by the Company in 2015.
assertion_b: The Property was sold to a third party in 2023.

assertion_a: Project Aurora was launched in March 2022.
assertion_b: Project Aurora was discontinued in December 2024 due to lack of market traction.

assertion_a: The Borrower's principal place of business was Boston, Massachusetts during the 2022 fiscal year.
assertion_b: The Borrower's principal place of business is San Francisco, California as of the date hereof.

assertion_a: Operating Subsidiary A was a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company through 2023.
assertion_b: Operating Subsidiary A is currently 51% owned by the Company, with 49% held by Strategic Partner B.

assertion_a: Prior to its take-private transaction in early 2024, the Company's publicly traded stock had never closed below $25 per share.
assertion_b: Following the Company's relisting in mid-2025, on October 15, 2025, the Company's stock closed at $18.40 per share.
notes: Two distinct trading regimes (pre-take-private vs post-relisting); both true at their stated scopes.

assertion_a: The Loan Agreement was originally executed on March 1, 2020.
assertion_b: The Amended and Restated Loan Agreement, effective April 1, 2024, replaces the original Loan Agreement in its entirety.

assertion_a: Annual Report for 2023: total assets were $215 million as of December 31, 2023.
assertion_b: Quarterly Report for Q3 2024: total assets are $248 million as of September 30, 2024.

assertion_a: The Settlement Agreement was reached on November 5, 2022, fully resolving the dispute.
assertion_b: A new dispute arose in 2024 concerning post-settlement obligations and is currently in arbitration.
notes: Old dispute resolved; new dispute is a distinct matter.
